titleEn: 1st Conference on Technology and Medicine
excerptEn: The Degree Hall of the Faculty of Medicine hosted, on Tuesday 18 November, the “1st Conference on Technology and Medicine”, organised by Juan A. Juanes Méndez, head of the research group on Advanced Medical Visualisation Systems (VisualMed System) and deputy director of the IUCE. The opening session was attended by Juan Manuel…
contentEn:
The Degree Hall of the Faculty of Medicine hosted, on Tuesday 18 November, the “1st Conference on Technology and Medicine”, organised by Juan A. Juanes Méndez, head of the research group on Advanced Medical Visualisation Systems (VisualMed System) and deputy director of the IUCE.

The opening session was attended by Juan Manuel Corchado, Vice-Rector for Research and director of the BISITE research group, and Francisco Javier García Criado, dean of the Faculty of Medicine.

The conference addressed the current state of technologies applied to the field of medicine in its various specialities. Different members of the VisualMed System group and of the BISITE group, both research groups recognised by the University of Salamanca, gave a demonstration of the various projects developed in technological environments of interest both for medical training and for clinical practice. During the session, attendees were able to try out and handle some of the devices presented.

Among the topics covered at the conference, the following stood out:

  • Virtual environment for ultrasound-guided regional anaesthetic blocks.
  • Technological environment for automatic monitoring of the retina using the ocular fundus.
  • Technological doctor-patient communication systems for the monitoring and control of conditions. Portable Medical Unit. Home Care. Azteca Project. Brain Controller.
  • Visual microarray analysis. DNA gene expression.
  • Ambulatory monitoring of the electrocardiographic record. EKG Mobile Project.
  • Augmented Reality with wearable devices, in Human Anatomy.

titleEn: Opening of TEEM'14
excerptEn: The second edition of the international conference “Technological Ecosystems for Enhancing Multiculturality” was opened this morning by the Vice-Rector for Research and Transfer of the University of Salamanca, Juan Manuel Corchado; the Director General for Educational Innovation and Teacher Training, Mª del Pilar González García; the director of the University Institute of Education…
contentEn:
The second edition of the international conference “Technological Ecosystems for Enhancing Multiculturality” was opened this morning by the Vice-Rector for Research and Transfer of the University of Salamanca, Juan Manuel Corchado; the Director General for Educational Innovation and Teacher Training, Mª del Pilar González García; the director of the University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca, María José Rodríguez Conde; and Francisco José García Peñalvo, Conference Chair.

The conference can be followed live at the following URL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q-iPm6tlff4

titleEn: Technological Ecosystems for Enhancing Multiculturality (TEEM'14) conference
excerptEn: On 1-3 October 2014 the IUCE of the University of Salamanca will host the second edition of the International Conference Technological Ecosystems for Enhancing Multiculturality (TEEM'14 – http://teemconference.eu). This edition strengthens its multicultural nature by combining different aspects of technology applied to the development of the knowledge society, with…
contentEn:
On 1-3 October 2014 the IUCE of the University of Salamanca will host the second edition of the International Conference Technological Ecosystems for Enhancing Multiculturality (TEEM’14 – http://teemconference.eu).

This edition strengthens its multicultural nature by combining different aspects of technology applied to the development of the knowledge society, with particular attention to topics related to education and learning. The conference is thus divided into the following sessions:

  • Innovation in Health Sciences Education
  • Educational Innovation
  • Gamification ecosystems
  • Dealing with complexity: Educational data and tools for Learning Analytics
  • Communication, Education and Health Promotion
  • Mobile Apps and computational systems as learning tools
  • Software As A Service (SaaS) in learning processes
  • Evaluation in Education and Guidance
  • New publishing and scientific communication ways: Electronic edition, digital educational resources, Open Access Repositories and Open Linked Data
  • Transferring knowledge and experiences from informal to formal learning contexts
  • Project presentations
  • Doctoral Consortium

All the thematic sessions share the aim of moving away from the more traditional academic conference format, seeking ways to discuss and improve ongoing and future research with the constructive feedback of the participants. The proceedings of this conference will be published as a volume of the ACM International Conference Proceedings Series and, therefore, in the ACM Digital Library. In addition, special issues will be organised in high-impact journals to extend the best papers and to give an opportunity to publish new work that may arise as a result of collaboration between the participants. The up-to-date list of journals is available at http://teemconference.eu/special-issues/.

titleEn: ECER Porto 2014
excerptEn: Ana Pérez Escoda and María José Rodríguez Conde, director of the IUCE, attended the ECER 2014 international conference “The Past, the Present and Future of Educational Research in Europe”, organised by the European Education Research Association (EERA) in Porto on 2-5 September.

titleEn: Final course of the 2014 General Teaching Staff Training Programme
excerptEn: Throughout the second semester the IUCE has been delivering the 2014 Teaching Training Plan for the Teaching Staff of the University of Salamanca, an initiative of the Vice-Rectorate for Teaching which brings together the institutional programme of initial and continuing training aimed at all teaching and research staff. Over these months the topics addressed have included, among others, methodologies for university…
contentEn:
Throughout the second semester the IUCE has been delivering the 2014 Teaching Training Plan for the Teaching Staff of the University of Salamanca, an initiative of the Vice-Rectorate for Teaching which brings together the institutional programme of initial and continuing training aimed at all teaching and research staff.

Over these months the topics addressed have included, among others, methodologies for university teaching, strategies for improving teaching quality, approaches to managing and developing e-learning environments, and computing and audiovisual resources for academic activity.

On 22 and 23 July, led by Albert Sangrà, academic director of the UNESCO Chair in Education and Technology for Social Change (Universitat Oberta de Catalunya), the final course of the General Teaching Staff Training Programme is taking place under the title “MOOCs in University Teaching”.

titleEn: Working session on the research projects carried out in the “Researchers' School”
excerptEn: On 18 June the IUCE, together with the Centre for Advanced Teacher Training of Castilla y León (CSFP), is organising a review session on the research projects carried out by 5 teams of pre-school, primary and secondary school teachers from Castilla y León within the educational innovation project “Researchers' School…
contentEn:
On 18 June the IUCE, together with the Centre for Advanced Teacher Training of Castilla y León (CSFP), is organising a review session on the research projects carried out by 5 teams of pre-school, primary and secondary school teachers from Castilla y León within the educational innovation project “Researchers' School Programme”.

This programme has launched the first Researchers' School through three training phases. A face-to-face, theoretical phase, held on 13 and 14 March, which served as a source of knowledge and an introduction to research, as well as a starting point for the project. Within it, five working groups were set up, covering five different themes. An online collaborative phase, run through the CSFP's virtual classroom, which has consisted of exchange and group learning under the coordination of a member of the IUCE research team. And finally a practical phase of experimentation, learning and practical application in the classroom, to test the link between research and teacher training and teachers' professional development.

titleEn: PIIECYL 2014: Experiences in Non-University Research
excerptEn: Last Friday, 30 May 2014, the IUCE had the pleasure of organising, together with the University of Salamanca and the Regional Government of Castilla y León, the presentation and awards day of the Research and Innovation Awards in ESO, Baccalaureate and Vocational Training, promoted by the Regional Government of Castilla y…
contentEn:
Last Friday, 30 May 2014, the IUCE had the pleasure of organising, together with the University of Salamanca and the Regional Government of Castilla y León, the presentation and awards day of the Research and Innovation Awards in ESO, Baccalaureate and Vocational Training, promoted by the Regional Government of Castilla y León itself.

The event took place in the Aula Minor of the Hospedería Fonseca of the University of Salamanca, and drew a large attendance of ESO, Baccalaureate and Vocational Training students from the various schools of Castilla y León that took part in the final phase of these Awards, as well as various members of the University, the Scientific Panel that assessed and decided the awards, and a number of distinguished guests, most notably the Regional Minister of Education of Castilla y León, Mr Juan José Mateos, the Secretary General of the University of Salamanca, Ms María Luisa Calvo, and the Director General for Educational Innovation and Teacher Training of the Regional Government of Castilla y León, Ms Mª del Pilar González García.

In addition to participation in situ, through the students' presentations of their work, the general public was involved in two ways: one, by providing live streaming of the event on YouTube, through which the various schools or anyone interested could follow the presentations and the full ceremony; and the second, by inviting people to express their opinion and give a vote to the projects they liked best on the social network Twitter. To this end, the event had a global hashtag (#piiecyl2014) and each project in turn was assigned a label or hashtag and every message or tweet sent with that tag counted as a vote that the Scientific Panel could use as a way of breaking ties in the judging of some of the awards. It is worth noting that the event was the 3rd Trending Topic (one of the most talked-about subjects on the social network Twitter) for more than 3 hours that morning, thus amply fulfilling the task of involving the general public in the dynamics of the day.


[11:26] TTs España (1/2): 1) #CosasQueDebesSaberDeTuiter 2) #ESOsueño 3) #piiecyl2014 4) #pccomponentes 5) #SerCanario

— TT España Bot (beta) (@ttesbot) May 30, 2014

 

For more information about these Awards and the day for the defence of the projects and the presentation of the Awards, please see their website: http://piiecyl.usal.es/

titleEn: Laura Briz at the University of Salamanca science outreach competition
excerptEn: Laura Briz Ponce, a student on the Education in the Knowledge Society doctoral programme, run within the University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca, has had her work selected for the University of Salamanca science outreach competition. The work is entitled “Analysis of…
contentEn:
Laura Briz Ponce, a student on the Education in the Knowledge Society doctoral programme, run within the University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca, has had her work selected for the University of Salamanca science outreach competition. The work is entitled “Analysis of the effectiveness of mhealth applications on mobile devices in the field of medical training”, prepared under the supervision of Professors Juan A. Juanes Méndez (deputy director of the Institute) and Francisco J. García Peñalvo (coordinator of the doctoral programme).

The work presented by Laura Briz seeks to shed light on the acceptance, satisfaction and appraisal of the use of mobile devices (mobility) in healthcare education. It also aims to design a quality protocol from the user's point of view, to serve as a certification for mobile applications in medical training.

The exhibition shows the public the results of the work carried out by the young researchers of our university.

The University of Salamanca science outreach competition received 65 entries, five more than in 2013. Of these, 25 have been selected.

They are works from a wide range of specialities, from astronomy to botany, by way of sociology and engineering, produced by Master's and doctoral students of the University of Salamanca.

titleEn: Cooperative seminar organised by CINAIC 2014
excerptEn: María José Rodríguez Conde, director of the University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca (IUCE), is taking part in the cooperative seminar “Educational Innovation: In search of the lost indicators”, which is taking place on 6 and 7 May in Zaragoza. This seminar, organised by the Congreso INternacional sobre Aprendizaje, Innovación y…
contentEn:
María José Rodríguez Conde, director of the University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca (IUCE), is taking part in the cooperative seminar “Educational Innovation: In search of the lost indicators”, which is taking place on 6 and 7 May in Zaragoza.

This seminar, organised by the Congreso INternacional sobre Aprendizaje, Innovación y Competitividad (CINAIC 2014), aims to analyse the state of educational innovation and, in particular, its indicators. Indicators make it possible to classify, measure and organise educational innovation, as well as to facilitate its transfer.

More information is available at the following link http://138.4.83.162/congreso/cinaic/sic/.

The deadline…
contentEn:

More information: http://web.ua.es/es/ice/jornadas-redes-2014/

titleEn: The IUCE takes part in the ‘Researchers' School Programme’
excerptEn: The Regional Ministry of Education, acting through the Directorate General for Educational Innovation and Teacher Training, has organised, with the collaboration of the Centre for Advanced Teacher Training and the support of the University Institute of Education Sciences of Salamanca, two working sessions aimed at drawing up a frame of reference for…
contentEn:
The Regional Ministry of Education, acting through the Directorate General for Educational Innovation and Teacher Training, has organised, with the collaboration of the Centre for Advanced Teacher Training and the support of the University Institute of Education Sciences of Salamanca, two working sessions aimed at drawing up a frame of reference for the development of the educational innovation project “Researchers' School Programme”.

The ‘Researchers' School Programme’ consists of three phases. The theoretical phase, delivered by the team of the University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca (IUCE) and held on 13 and 14 March in Valladolid, is a face-to-face training module in which the conceptual framework needed for the development of the project is established; in it, the research teams were formed and the topics of the research initiatives were defined.

The second phase will be practical and will consist of experimentation with, learning from and application in the classroom or in the educational field of the training previously received, by means of a research initiative carried out by the participants in working teams. This phase will take place at each participant's workplace and will be mentored by the IUCE team of the University of Salamanca, with the coordination of the CSFP.

Finally, a collaborative phase will take place, based on exchange, group learning and help in resolving difficulties and challenges. This phase runs concurrently with the practical phase (from 17 March to 30 May) and is completed with a final reflection on the research carried out and the competences acquired, taking place online through the virtual classroom of the Centre for Advanced Teacher Training.

Lastly, once the project has been implemented and developed by the selected teachers, the Directorate General for Educational Innovation and Teacher Training, with the collaboration of the CSFP, will carry out a final evaluation of the project. The results of this assessment will serve to extend this training experience to a greater number of teachers in the future.

Source: http://www.salamanca24horas.com/universidad/106340-la-junta-fomentara-la-investigacion-e-innovacion-educativa-en-la-docencia

titleEn: Visit from the Centro Regional de Profesores del Litoral, Salto, Uruguay
excerptEn: A delegation from the Centro Regional de Profesores del Litoral, Salto, Uruguay, is visiting the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of Salamanca to discuss the joint TIC USAL CERP project and to consider new avenues of collaboration between the two institutions. On the occasion of the forthcoming eighth anniversary of the initiative…
contentEn:
A delegation from the Centro Regional de Profesores del Litoral, Salto, Uruguay, is visiting the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) of the University of Salamanca to discuss the joint TIC USAL CERP project and to consider new avenues of collaboration between the two institutions.

To mark the forthcoming eighth anniversary of the university development cooperation initiative with Uruguay, the director Víctor Pizzichillo and the lecturer Rosa Durán, of the Teacher Training Centre, are visiting the University of Salamanca.

At the Salamanca institution they were received by the Vice-Rector for Internationalisation, Mª Ángeles Serrano, and by the director of the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE), Mª José Rodríguez Conde, with whom they held several meetings to advance future collaborative projects.

The TIC USAL CERP project began in 2006 and focuses on digitalisation processes and the training of teaching staff in new technologies.

Source: Dicyt http://www.dicyt.com/noticias/colaboracion-para-impulsar-las-nuevas-tecnologias-en-educacion

titleEn: Research and Innovation Awards in Compulsory Secondary Education, Baccalaureate and Vocational Training
excerptEn: The University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca will host, on 30 May 2014, the first presentation ceremony of the Research and Innovation Awards in Compulsory Secondary Education, Baccalaureate and Vocational Training in the Community of Castilla y León. The research and innovation projects…
contentEn:
The University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca will host, on 30 May 2014, the first presentation ceremony of the Research and Innovation Awards in Compulsory Secondary Education, Baccalaureate and Vocational Training in the Community of Castilla y León.

The selected research and innovation projects will be presented publicly, with a maximum of 15 minutes each, before a jury that will choose the three best, one for each educational stage: compulsory secondary education, baccalaureate and vocational training.

More information about the call in the BOCYL of 8 January 2014: http://bocyl.jcyl.es/boletines/2014/01/08/pdf/BOCYL-D-08012014-9.pdf

titleEn: A selection of images from the TEEM'13 conference
excerptEn: The first edition of the Technological Ecosystems for Enhancing Multiculturality (TEEM'13) conference, which took place in November at the University Institute of Education Sciences, enjoyed extensive technological coverage in order to give the widest possible dissemination to the various events and sessions held within it. The sessions…
contentEn: The first edition of the Technological Ecosystems for Enhancing Multiculturality (TEEM’13) conference, which took place in November at the University Institute of Education Sciences, enjoyed extensive technological coverage in order to give the widest possible dissemination to the various events and sessions held within it. The most notable sessions were streamed live and all the presentations were recorded, and the videos of each of them will be available over the course of January. A large number of photographs were also taken, of which a small selection is available at the following link: http://www.flickr.com/photos/grialusal/sets/72157639555981545/

contentEn:

For more information see the following link http://innovaciondocente.sitios.uva.es/?q=node/137

titleEn: The Director General for Educational Innovation and Teacher Training and the Rector of the University of Salamanca will open the TEEM'13 conference
excerptEn: The University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca will host, on 14, 15 and 16 November, TEEM (Technological Ecosystems for Enhancing Multiculturality, http://teemconference.eu), an international conference covering topics related to the knowledge society and multiculturality, which will also accommodate the closing of the European ICT project…
contentEn:
The University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca will host, on 14, 15 and 16 November, TEEM (Technological Ecosystems for Enhancing Multiculturality, http://teemconference.eu), an international conference covering topics related to the knowledge society and multiculturality, which will also accommodate the closing event of the European ICT (KA 3) project Tagging, Recognition and Acknowledgment of Informal Learning ExpeRiences (TRAILER, http://trailerproject.eu), and the kick-off meeting of the European Erasmus project Virtual Alliances for Learning Society (VALS), both belonging to the Lifelong Learning Programme.

The opening ceremony will be attended by the Director General for Educational Innovation and Teacher Training, Regional Ministry of Education, Regional Government of Castilla y León, Mª del Pilar González García; the Rector of the University of Salamanca, Dr Daniel Hernández Ruipérez; the director of the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE), Dr Mª José Rodríguez Conde; and the director of the research group in InterAction and eLearning (GRIAL), Dr Francisco J. García Peñalvo.

The aim of this event is to create a community of researchers who exchange experiences and discuss ways of fostering multiculturality from the interdisciplinary perspective characteristic of the Knowledge Society. In this regard, TEEM is linked to the Education in the Knowledge Society Doctoral Programme of the University of Salamanca (http://www.usal.es/webusal/node/30026).

For this first edition, a series of thematic sessions has been organised with the shared aim of moving away from the more traditional academic conference format, focusing instead on discussing and improving ongoing research and on seeking new prospects for joint collaboration, with the active participation of more than 100 delegates of various nationalities.

Within the conference, specifically on Saturday 16 November, a session will also be held for sharing experiences from national and international research projects, with particular emphasis on European projects, related to the knowledge society (http://teemconference.eu/projects). This session will feature Alfredo Mateos, director of the Research Results Transfer Office (OTRI) of the University of Salamanca, Mª Goretti Alonso de Castro, coordinator of the Evaluation and Monitoring Area of the Autonomous Body for European Educational Programmes, and a representative of the Agency for Innovation, Business Finance and Internationalisation (ADE) of the Regional Government of Castilla y León.

titleEn: Technological Ecosystems for Enhancing Multiculturality International Conference (TEEM 2013)
excerptEn: The first edition of the Technological Ecosystems for Enhancing Multiculturality International Conference (TEEM – http://teemconference.eu/) will be held at the Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE – http://iuce.usal.es) of the University of Salamanca on 15-16 November 2013. The aim of this event is to create a community of researchers who exchange…
contentEn:
The first edition of the Technological Ecosystems for Enhancing Multiculturality International Conference (TEEM – http://teemconference.eu/) will be held at the Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE – http://iuce.usal.es) of the University of Salamanca on 15-16 November 2013.

The aim of this event is to create a community of researchers who exchange experiences and discuss ways of fostering multiculturality from the interdisciplinary perspective characteristic of the Knowledge Society. In this regard, TEEM is linked to the Education in the Knowledge Society Doctoral Programme of the University of Salamanca (http://www.usal.es/webusal/node/30026).

For this first edition a series of thematic sessions has been organised, each with its own topics and call for papers (click on the corresponding track for more information):

  • Track 1. Doctoral Consortium
  • Track 2. Technological advances and teaching innovation applied to Health Sciences education
  • Track 3. Communication and cultural diversity
  • Track 4. Science 2.0 supported by Open Access Repositories and Open Linked Data
  • Track 5. A promised land for educational decision making? Present and future of learning analytics
  • Track 6. A robot in the classroom
  • Track 7. Formal and informal learning experiences in multicultural scopes
  • Track 8. Application of Mobile and Software As A Service (SAAS) in learning processes
  • Track 9. Educational innovation
  • Track 10. New publishing and scientific communication ways: Electronic edition and digital educational resources
  • Track 11. 4th International Workshop on Software Engineering for E-learning (ISELEAR’13)

All the thematic sessions share the aim of moving away from the more traditional academic conference format, seeking ways to discuss and improve ongoing and future research with the constructive feedback of the participants.

The proceedings of this conference will be published as a volume of the ACM International Conference Proceedings Series and, therefore, in the ACM Digital Library.

In addition, special issues will be organised in 12 journals to extend (by at least 60%) the best papers and to give an opportunity to publish new work that may arise as a result of collaboration between the participants. The up-to-date list of journals is available at http://teemconference.eu/special-issues/.
